You will be provided a description of problem and then you should generate the right code to solve that problem.

You should ensure that the code is complete and runnable without modification, including all necessary imports.

Please generate code to solve the following task:

给你一个长度为 `n` 的整数数组 `nums`。

如果存在索引 `0 < p < q < n − 1`，使得数组满足以下条件，则称其为 **三段式数组（trionic）**：

- `nums[0...p]` **严格** 递增，
- `nums[p...q]` **严格** 递减，
- `nums[q...n − 1]` **严格** 递增。

如果 `nums` 是三段式数组，返回 `true`；否则，返回 `false`。

 

**示例 1:**

**输入:** nums = [1,3,5,4,2,6]

**输出:** true

**解释:**

选择 `p = 2`, `q = 4`：

- `nums[0...2] = [1, 3, 5]` 严格递增 (`1 < 3 < 5`)。
- `nums[2...4] = [5, 4, 2]` 严格递减 (`5 > 4 > 2`)。
- `nums[4...5] = [2, 6]` 严格递增 (`2 < 6`)。

**示例 2:**

**输入:** nums = [2,1,3]

**输出:** false

**解释:**

无法选出能使数组满足三段式要求的 `p` 和 `q` 。

 

**提示:**

- `3 <= n <= 100`
- `-1000 <= nums[i] <= 1000`

======
You must strictly follow this signature:
-spec solve(list[int]) -> bool.
solve(list[int]) ->

Target language: Erlang

Requirements:
1. You may include auxiliary/helper code before or after the required signature.
2. All import/include statements (e.g., `import` in Python, `#include` in C/C++) must be placed at the very top of the file.
3. Preserve the provided signature exactly as given. Do not modify parameter names, types, or return types.
4. Ensure the final code is complete, self-contained, and directly runnable.
5. Return ONLY the raw code. Do not include explanations, markdown backticks, or conversational text.

You are generating Erlang code. Generate only the function solve/{arity}. Do NOT include -module, -export, or main. Use binary strings (<<"...">>) for strings. Lists are standard Erlang lists. Return values as native terms.

__data__.output:
```erlang
-spec solve(nums :: list()) -> boolean().
solve(nums) ->
    case find_p(nums) of
        {p, Rest} ->
            case find_q(Rest, p) of
                {q, Last} ->
                    check_increasing( lists:nth(p + 1, nums), lists:nth(p, nums), lists:nth(q, nums), Last );
                false ->
                    false
            end;
        false ->
            false
    end.

find_p(nums) ->
    P = find_p_helper(nums, 0, 1, 1),
    case P of
        {p, Rest} ->
            {p, Rest};
        false ->
            false
    end.

find_p_helper([H|T], P, Inc, _Val) when P < length(nums) - 2 ->
    case Inc of
        true ->
            case T of
                [H2|T2] when H < H2 ->
                    find_p_helper(T2, P + 1, true, H2);
                _ ->
                    false
            end;
        false ->
            false
    end.

find_q(Rest, P) ->
    Q = find_q_helper(Rest, P, 0, 1, 1),
    case Q of
        {q, Last} ->
            {q, Last};
        false ->
            false
    end.

find_q_helper([H|T], P, Q, Dec, _Val) when Q < length(Rest) - 1 ->
    case Dec of
        true ->
            case T of
                [H2|T2] when H > H2 ->
                    find_q_helper(T2, P, Q + 1, true, H2);
                _ ->
                    false
            end;
        false ->
            false
    end.

check_increasing(Val, Prev, Next, Last) ->
    case Next of
        Val ->
            check_increasing(Next, Val, Last, Last);
        _ ->
            case Last of
                Val ->
                    true;
                _ ->
                    false
            end
    end.

check_increasing(Val, Prev, Next, Last) ->
    case Next of
        Val ->
            check_increasing(Next, Val, Last, Last);
        _ ->
            case Last of
                Val ->
                    true;
                _ ->
                    false
            end
    end.
```

-spec solve(list[int]) -> bool.
solve(list[int]) ->

Target language: Erlang

Requirements:
1. You may include auxiliary/helper code before or after the required signature.
2. All import/include statements (e.g., `import` in Python, `#include` in C/C++) must be placed at the very top of the file.
3. Preserve the provided signature exactly as given. Do not modify parameter names, types, or return types.

__data__.output:
```erlang
-spec solve(nums :: list()) -> boolean().
solve(nums) ->
    case find_p(nums) of
        {p, Rest} ->
            case find_q(Rest, p) of
                {q, Final} ->
                    is_increasing( lists:nthtail(p + 1, nums), q, Final);
                error ->
                    false
            end;
        error ->
            false
    end.

find_p(nums) ->
    find_p(nums, 0, 0, true).

find_p([_, H | _], P, _, false) ->
    {P, nums};
find_p([_, H | T], P, _, true) ->
    case H > lists:nth(P + 1, nums) of
        true ->
            find_p(T, P + 1, H, false);
        false ->
            error
    end.

find_q(Rest, P) ->
    find_q(Rest, P, 0, true).

find_q([_, H | T], P, _, false) ->
    {P, Rest};
find_q([_, H | T], P, _, true) ->
    case H < lists:nth(P + 1, Rest) of
        true ->
            find_q(T, P, H, false);
        false ->
            error
    end.

is_increasing(List, Q, Final) ->
    case lists:split(Q, List) of
        {_, Rest} ->
            case is_increasing_rest(Rest, Final) of
                true ->
                    true;
                false ->
                    false
            end;
        error ->
            false
    end.

is_increasing_rest([H | T], Final) ->
    case lists:member(H, Final) of
        true ->
            case T of
                [] ->
                    true;
                [_ | _] ->
                    is_increasing_rest(T, Final)
            end;
        false ->
            false
    end.
```

- `nums[q...n − 1]` **严格** 递增。

-spec solve(list[int]) -> bool.
solve(list[int]) ->

Target language: Erlang

Requirements:
1. You may include auxiliary/helper code before or after the required signature.
2. All import/include statements (e.g., `import` in Python, `#include` in C/C++) must be placed at the very top of the file.
3. Preserve the provided signature exactly as given. Do not modify parameter names, types, or return types.

__data__.output:
```erlang
-spec solve(nums :: list()) -> boolean().
solve(nums) ->
    case find_p(nums) of
        error -> false;
        P -> 
            case find_q(nums, P) of
                error -> false;
                Q -> check_trionic(nums, P, Q)
            end
    end.

find_p(nums) ->
    P = lists:foldl(fun(X, Acc) -> 
        case Acc of
            false -> false;
            P -> 
                case lists:member(X, lists:sublist(nums, P)) of
                    true -> false;
                    false -> P
                end
        end
    end, false, lists:seq(1, length(nums) - 2)).

find_q(nums, P) ->
    Q = lists:foldl(fun(X, Acc) -> 
        case Acc of
            false -> false;
            Q -> 
                case lists:member(X, lists:sublist(nums, Q)) of
                    true -> false;
                    false -> Q
                end
        end
    end, false, lists:seq(P + 1, length(nums) - 1)).

check_trionic(nums, P, Q) ->
    Inc = lists:sublist(nums, 1, P),
    Dec = lists:sublist(nums, P, Q),
    Inc2 = lists:sublist(nums, Q, length(nums)),
    check_increasing(Inc) andalso check_decreasing(Dec) andalso check_increasing(Inc2).

check_increasing(List) ->
    case lists:all(fun(X, Y) -> X < Y end, List) of
        true -> true;
        false -> false
    end.

check_decreasing(List) ->
    case lists:all(fun(X, Y) -> X > Y end, List) of
        true -> true;
        false -> false
    end.
```

```erlang
-spec solve(nums :: list()) -> boolean().
solve(nums) ->
    N = length(nums),
    case find_p(nums, 1, 1) of
        {p, true} ->
            case find_q(nums, p, 1, 1) of
                {q, true} ->
                    true;
                _ ->
                    false
            end;
        _ ->
            false
    end.

find_p(nums, P, Start) ->
    case check_increasing(nums, Start, P) of
        true ->
            {P, true};
        false ->
            find_p(nums, P + 1, Start)
    end.

check_increasing(nums, Start, P) ->
    case Start < P of
        true ->
            case nums[Start] < nums[P] of
                true ->
                    true;
                false ->
                    false
            end;
        false ->
            false
    end.

find_q(nums, P, Q, Start) ->
    case check_decreasing(nums, P, Q) of
        true ->
            case check_increasing(nums, Q, length(nums) - 1) of
                true ->
                    {Q, true};
                false ->
                    false
            end;
        false ->
            find_q(nums, P, Q + 1, Start)
    end.

check_decreasing(nums, P, Q) ->
    case P < Q of
        true ->
            case nums[P] > nums[Q] of
                true ->
                    true;
                false ->
                    false
            end;
        false ->
            false
    end.
```

```erlang
-spec solve(nums :: list(integer())) -> boolean().
solve(nums) ->
    case find_p(nums) of
        {p, Rest} ->
            case find_q(Rest, p) of
                {q, FinalRest} ->
                    is_increasing( lists:sublist(nums, p+1, q-p), true );
                false ->
                    false
            end;
        false ->
            false
    end.

find_p(nums) ->
    find_p(nums, 0, 0, 0).

find_p([_], _, _, _) ->
    false;
find_p([H, T, Next], P, _, _) when H < T, T < Next ->
    {P, [Next]};
find_p([H, T, Next], P, Prev, _) when H < T, T > Next ->
    {P, [Next]};
find_p([H, T, Next], P, Prev, _) when H < T, T =< Next ->
    false;
find_p([H, T, Next], P, Prev, _) when H >= T ->
    false.

find_q(Rest, P) ->
    find_q(Rest, P, 0, 0).

find_q([_], _, _, _) ->
    false;
find_q([H, T, Next], P, Q, _) when H > T, T > Next ->
    {Q, [Next]};
find_q([H, T, Next], P, Q, _) when H > T, T =< Next ->
    false;
find_q([H, T, Next], P, Q, _) when H =< T ->
    false.

is_increasing(List, true) ->
    is_increasing(List, true, 1, 1).

is_increasing([H], _, _, _) ->
    true;
is_increasing([H, T], _, _, _) when H < T ->
    true;
is_increasing([H, T, Next], _, _, _) when H < T, T < Next ->
    true;
is_increasing([H, T, Next], _, _, _) when H < T, T > Next ->
    false;
is_increasing([H, T, Next], _, _, _) when H >= T ->
    false.
```
